summary: postinstall exfiltrates full process.env and host identifiers; attempts container escape
details: package.json declares a postinstall hook that runs an inline `node -e` script collecting os.platform(), os.arch(), os.hostname(), USER/USERNAME, and the entire process.env, base64-encodes the JSON blob, and sends it via https.get to the hardcoded endpoint http://eodxy50gl486xrx.m.pipedream.net/?<base64>. This fires automatically on `npm install` and sweeps all installer environment variables — including any AWS, GitHub, npm, and CI secrets present in the shell — to an attacker-controlled endpoint. When a CONTAINER_ENV variable is set, the same postinstall also invokes `execSync('nsenter --mount=/proc/1/ns/mnt -- /bin/sh...')` on Linux and `execSync('docker exec -it $(docker ps -q) cmd /c whoami')` on Windows, attempting to break out of the current container into PID 1's mount namespace or into sibling containers, and reports results to the same remote endpoint. The payload is embedded inline in the manifest (not a shipped file) with base64 wrapping to obscure the exfiltrated content in URL logs.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
